test.command: BROWNIE_NETWORK=mainnet TYPEDENVS_SHUTUP=1 .venv/bin/python -c "import concurrent.futures.process as cfp; ...; pytest tests/test_constants.py -x -p no:pytest_ethereum -W ignore --timeout 120"
test.notes: 6/6 passed. UnicodeDecodeError in brownie's teardown phase is pre-existing and unrelated to mission changes.

typecheck.notes: 100 errors across v2.py and v3.py — all pre-existing technical debt. No new errors introduced by mission changes. Mission-specific additions (ROUTING_TOKENS in constants.py, new methods following existing patterns) follow the same type-annotation patterns as surrounding code.

lint.notes: 6 files need reformatting (setup.py, y/_db/utils/utils.py, y/prices/utils/sense_check.py, y/prices/utils/ypriceapi.py, y/utils/raw_calls.py, y/contracts.py) — all pre-existing before the mission. Mission-added tests/prices/dex/test_v2_multipool.py now passes black --check (fixed in fix-multihop-scrutiny-findings).

reviewsSummary.notes: Round 2 only reviewed fix-multihop-scrutiny-findings (the fix for v2-multipool-candidates which failed in round 1). All round 1 reviews are carried forward: routing-tokens-constants (pass), v3-amount-aware-liquidity (pass), v3-multihop-expansion (pass). Fix review passed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a coding convention: 'When calling async generators on ASyncGenericBase subclasses, do NOT pass sync=True/False — only @a_sync.a_sync-decorated methods support that kwarg. Use `async for item in self.method()` directly.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v2-multipool-candidates had a blocking runtime bug (TypeError) from passing sync=False to an async generator (top_pools). The fix-multihop-scrutiny-findings worker correctly fixed this, and architecture.md library note has been added. A worker-facing rule in AGENTS.md would prevent recurrence. Same observation carried from round 1 synthesis — still not actioned.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note about the intentional divergence between ROUTING_TOKENS L2 addresses and the existing bridged contract objects: 'ROUTING_TOKENS uses native token addresses for L2 chains, not the existing bridged usdc/usdt Contract objects, because native tokens have deeper liquidity.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: routing-tokens-constants review flagged this divergence as undocumented, which could confuse future maintainers. Carried from round 1.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In python-lib-worker skill's 'Finding Test Tokens' section, add: 'Check mission AGENTS.md for a pre-documented test tokens table BEFORE searching on-chain.' v3-multihop-expansion worker used MIC (which failed) instead of the SIX token documented in mission AGENTS.md.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both v3-multihop-expansion and v2-multipool-candidates reviewers noted that the mission AGENTS.md 'Useful Test Tokens' section was underutilized. Carried from round 1.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In python-lib-worker skill, add a note about the async generator sync kwarg limitation as a common pitfall: 'Before calling any method with sync=False, verify it is decorated with @a_sync.a_sync (not a plain async generator). Async generators on ASyncGenericBase get ASyncGeneratorFunction wrapping which does not support sync kwarg.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v2-multipool-candidates blocking bug caused by this exact mistake. Round 2 fix-multihop-scrutiny-findings reviewer also noted this convention gap still exists in AGENTS.md.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a `test-slow` command that appends --runslow to the test-full command. The --runslow pytest option added in conftest.py is not exposed in services.yaml, making it hard for workers to discover how to run slow V3 integration tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v3-amount-aware-liquidity reviewer noted that @pytest.mark.slow tests are skipped by default and --runslow is undocumented in services.yaml. Carried from round 1.

rejectedObservations.observation: v3.py USDC skip uses string comparison instead of Address-type comparison
rejectedObservations.reason: non-blocking style note — consistent with pre-existing patterns in surrounding code, no functional impact
